article.title: The Rise of Hydroponics
article.content: As city spaces become more restricted, soil-less farming techniques like hydroponics have moved from niche hobbies to mainstream solutions. By using nutrient-rich water, these systems use 90% less water than traditional farming.

article.title: Vertical Forests: High-Rise Habitats
article.content: Architects are now integrating thousands of trees and shrubs into the facades of skyscrapers. These "Vertical Forests" help regulate building temperatures and filter urban pollutants.

article.title: Community Gardens and Mental Health
article.content: Recent studies suggest that residents involved in local community gardens report a 30% decrease in stress levels. The act of tending to plants fosters a sense of belonging and environmental stewardship.
